_说明: 可复用 Prompt 区块库。prompt-builder.js 按文章类型从此文件按需拼接 Prompt 各部分。所有区块均为英文指令，直接注入生成器上下文。
_使用方式: 每个字段对应 Prompt 中一个独立区块。字段内容应直接可嵌入 Prompt，不需要再改写。
authorDisclaimer: Every article must end with an 'About this article' block using the author profile from author.json. Use the exact name, title, and persona fields. The block must state the content basis (hands-on kit testing, buyer questions, direct product evaluation). Include the reviewDate field from author.json. Format: --- / About this article / Written by [author.name], [author.title] at [site.siteName]. / [One sentence from author.persona describing the content basis.] / Where conclusions are observational rather than verified, this is noted in the text. / Last reviewed: [author.reviewDate] / ---
observationalClaim: Distinguish between verified facts and observational conclusions at all times. Verified facts (supported by product specs, manufacturer data, or documented buyer records): use 'According to [source]...', 'The product page states...', 'Our records show...'. Observational conclusions (based on our evaluation, testing, or buyer feedback): use 'In our experience...', 'Based on the kits we've tested...', 'Most builders we've heard from report...'. Never mix the two formats in the same sentence. Never state an observational conclusion as a verified fact.
internalLinkRule: Apply internal links according to the priority order in site.json → internalLinkPriority: pillarPages first, then categoryPages, then productPages, then blogPosts. Every article must include at least 2 links to pillarPages (from links.json → pillarPages[]). At least 1 link must go to a categoryPage. Every product name mentioned must be linked to its product page. Use descriptive anchor text containing the target keyword — never 'click here' or 'read more'. Maximum 5 internal links per article unless the article type is 'pillar' (max 8). Do not repeat the same URL more than once. The first pillarPage link should appear before the end of the second H2 section.
ctaStyle: Every article must end with a CTA section before the author block. CTA must: (1) link to a product page or category page relevant to the article topic, (2) use action-oriented anchor text that matches what the reader decided to do after reading, (3) avoid phrases like 'buy now', 'shop here', 'click here', 'limited time'. Preferred CTA patterns: 'Browse [category] kits →', 'See which kits include [feature] →', 'Find the right difficulty level for you →'. CTA must never appear as a standalone button placeholder — write it as natural sentence-level copy followed by a link.
outlineInstruction:
Before writing the article body, produce a structured outline in this exact format:

## OUTLINE: [slug]
**Keyword**: [primary keyword]
**Article Type**: [articletype]
**Working Title**: [proposed H1]
**Target Word Count**: [number]
**Reader Promise**: One sentence — what will the reader be able to decide or do after reading this?
**Sections**:
- H2: [section title] ([target word count for this section])
  - Key points to cover: [bullet list]
  - Internal links to place here: [URL + anchor text]
- (repeat for each H2)
**FAQ**: [list 3-5 questions from buyerFAQ[] relevant to this keyword]
**CTA**: [proposed CTA copy + target URL]
**QA Risks**: [list any risks from qa-rules.json hardFail[] or warnings[] that apply to this article]

Do not begin writing the article body until the outline has been confirmed.
articleFromOutlineInstruction: Write the article strictly following the confirmed outline. Do not add sections not in the outline. Do not remove sections from the outline. If a required section from article-types.json is missing from the outline, flag it before writing — do not silently skip it. Follow the word count targets per section. Place internal links at the positions specified in the outline. Insert FAQ questions from the outline's FAQ list exactly as written — do not paraphrase or reorder them.
brandVoiceRule:
Write in the voice defined by author.json → writingStyleNotes and site.json → writingStyle. Apply these constraints at all times:
- Sentence style: specific before general. Lead with a number, a step, a material name, a buyer's exact question, or a measurable outcome. Never lead with a feeling.
- Tone: deliberate and warm without sweetness. Warmth comes from precision, not exclamation marks.
- Paragraph length: maximum 4 sentences. Never stack two long paragraphs back to back.
- No stacked adjectives: one descriptive word per noun, maximum.
- No AI transition phrases: never use 'Furthermore', 'In conclusion', 'It's worth noting', 'At the end of the day', 'Dive in', 'Game-changing', 'Seamlessly'.
- Opening sentences: must use one of the three templates in article-types.json → openingTemplates[]. Never open with 'Have you ever...', 'Are you looking for...', 'As a [role]...', or a question.
productMentionRule:
Every time a specific kit or product is mentioned in the article body, follow these rules:
(1) State the difficulty level (Intermediate / Advanced) and estimated build time on first mention.
(2) Link the product name to its product page URL from links.json → productPages[].
(3) Do not use more than one emotional/sensory adjective per product per article.
(4) Never describe a kit as 'easy', 'simple', 'for everyone', 'beginner-friendly' without disclosing piece count and build time.
(5) Every product mention must reference at least one verifiable attribute (piece count, LED included, English instructions, build time range).
difficultyDisclosureRule:
Difficulty and time must be disclosed in every article that mentions a specific kit, before or at the point of first product mention. Use these standard labels:
- Intermediate: approximately 4–7 hours. Best split across two sessions.
- Advanced: approximately 8–15 hours. Plan for multiple sessions.
Never omit this disclosure. Never describe a kit as 'quick' or 'done in an afternoon' without a specific hour range. If the user is building their first kit, recommend Intermediate kits under 200 pieces and under 6 hours.
antiGenericWritingRule:
Reject these patterns at draft stage — do not include them in any output:
- Generic product intros: 'This beautiful kit...', 'This stunning miniature...', 'A magical experience...'
- Empty claims: 'high quality', 'premium craftsmanship', 'perfect for everyone', 'life-changing'
- Vague instructions: 'Assemble carefully', 'Make sure it fits' — every instruction must include a measurable action
- Emotional openers without data: 'If you love crafting...' — replace with a specific fact or observation
- Authority claims without basis: 'As experts in miniature kits...' — use author.json persona instead
- Listing features without buyer benefit: 'Includes LED' → 'Includes warm-white LED string — no separate purchase needed'
Every sentence must earn its place by giving the reader a decision-relevant fact, a specific observation, or an actionable instruction.
sourceOfTruthRule:
All factual claims in the article must be traceable to one of these sources:
(1) knowledge.json → authorityFacts[] — use verbatim or paraphrase closely
(2) links.json → productPages[] — product specifications
(3) author.json → storyBank[] — first-person observations (match useFor field to article type)
(4) knowledge.json → buyerFAQ[] — buyer questions and verified answers
If a claim cannot be traced to one of these sources, either omit it or mark it with an observational qualifier (see observationalClaim rule). Do not invent statistics, specifications, or buyer testimonials.
styleReferenceRule:
When style-reference.json is available for this site, use it as the HTML structure reference for all generated article output. Apply these rules:
(1) Use the site's article container class conventions from extractedStyle.articleContainer.
(2) Replicate heading hierarchy and class patterns from extractedStyle.typography.
(3) Use the sampleBlocks provided for FAQ sections, product cards, CTA blocks, and author blocks — do not create custom block structures when a sample exists.
(4) Do not output <head>, <body>, <style>, <script>, or any full-page HTML. Generate only CMS-embeddable article HTML starting from inside <article>.
(5) Do not output plain unstyled h1/h2/p-only content when the style reference provides richer reusable block structures.
(6) Prefer the site's existing class names over generic Tailwind or Bootstrap classes.
(7) If a required article section has no matching sampleBlock, output clean semantic HTML that does not conflict with the site's existing CSS conventions.
(8) Always check article-types.json → recommendedStyleBlocks for this article type and include those block patterns in the output.
